title: Kai and the Case of the Missing Rainbow
synopsis: When the rainbow disappears from the sky, young detective Kai teams up with his friends to solve the mystery using science and observation skills.

pages.text: Kai loved rainbows. He knew all seven colors by heart. Red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, and violet! Every day after rain, Kai would run outside to look for rainbows in the sky.

illustration.description: A cheerful young Asian boy with black hair and big brown eyes running outside, arms outstretched, looking up at the sky with excitement. He's wearing a bright red t-shirt and blue shorts. The background shows a sunny yard with puddles on the ground and blue sky with white fluffy clouds.
illustration.alt: Kai running outside excitedly looking for rainbows

pages.text: But today was different. It had rained all morning. The sun came out. Kai ran outside. But where was the rainbow? 'That's strange,' said Kai. 'There should be a rainbow here!'

illustration.description: Kai standing in his backyard looking puzzled and concerned, hand shading his eyes as he searches the bright blue sky. The sun is shining brightly, there are wet puddles reflecting light, but no rainbow visible. His expression shows confusion and determination to solve this mystery.
illustration.alt: Kai looking puzzled at the empty sky where a rainbow should be

pages.text: Kai's neighbor Zara came over. She had curly dark hair and loved science too. 'Hi Kai! Why do you look worried?' she asked. 'The rainbow is missing!' said Kai. 'We need to solve this mystery!'

illustration.description: Two children in a sunny backyard - Kai looking worried and Zara, a Black girl with beautiful curly dark hair and big expressive eyes, wearing a purple dress and yellow cardigan, approaching with a friendly, curious expression. The setting shows a colorful neighborhood with houses in the background.
illustration.alt: Kai talking to his neighbor Zara about the missing rainbow

pages.text: Kai and Zara made a list of clues. 'We have sun,' said Zara. 'We have water from the rain,' said Kai. 'But no rainbow!' Their friend Marcus joined them. He had freckles and red hair.

illustration.description: Three diverse children gathered around a small notebook, writing down clues. Kai is pointing at the sky, Zara is writing in the notebook, and Marcus, a boy with red hair, freckles, and green eyes wearing an orange shirt, is looking thoughtful. They're standing on wet grass with puddles around them.
illustration.alt: The three friends making a list of clues about the missing rainbow

pages.text: 'Maybe the rainbow is hiding?' asked Marcus. 'Rainbows don't hide,' laughed Zara. 'They're made of light!' Kai had an idea. 'Let's look for more clues. Maybe we need to make our own rainbow!'

illustration.description: The three children in animated discussion, with Marcus scratching his head looking confused, Zara laughing with her hands on her hips, and Kai with his finger pointed up having a eureka moment. Their expressions are very animated and the bright colors of their clothing pop against the sunny outdoor setting.
illustration.alt: The friends discussing theories about where the rainbow went

pages.text: They ran to Kai's house. His mom was watering plants with a garden hose. 'Mom!' called Kai. 'Can we borrow the hose? We're solving a rainbow mystery!' Mom smiled. 'Of course, little detectives!'

illustration.description: A warm scene showing Kai's mom, a kind Asian woman with long black hair in a ponytail, wearing a green gardening apron, smiling as she hands the garden hose to the excited children. The setting shows a lush garden with colorful flowers and plants, creating a vibrant and welcoming atmosphere.
illustration.alt: Kai's mom lending the garden hose to help solve the mystery

pages.text: Kai held the hose up high. Zara turned on the water. Marcus stood with his back to the sun. 'Look!' shouted Marcus. 'I can see colors in the water spray!' A beautiful rainbow appeared in the mist!

illustration.description: An exciting scene showing the three children successfully creating a rainbow with the garden hose. Water sprays in an arc through the air, creating a brilliant rainbow with all seven colors clearly visible. The children are jumping and pointing with joy, their faces lit up with wonder and accomplishment.
illustration.alt: The children creating their own rainbow with the garden hose

pages.text: 'We did it!' cheered Kai. 'But why didn't we see the rainbow in the sky?' asked Zara. Kai looked around. 'Oh! The big tree is blocking the sun! The rainbow WAS there, but the tree's shadow hid it from us!'

illustration.description: The children standing under a large oak tree with spreading branches, looking up and pointing at how the tree's shadow covers part of the sky. Kai has his detective expression on, with his finger pointing up at the tree branches. Sunbeams filter through the leaves, creating dappled light patterns.
illustration.alt: Kai realizing the tree was blocking their view of the rainbow

pages.text: The friends ran to the other side of the yard. There it was! A real rainbow arching across the sky! 'Mystery solved!' said Kai. 'We learned that rainbows need sun AND water AND the right spot to see them!'

illustration.description: A triumphant final scene with all three children celebrating in an open part of the yard, arms raised in victory, with a magnificent natural rainbow stretching across the blue sky behind them. Their expressions show pure joy and accomplishment. The colors are vibrant and the mood is celebratory and magical.
illustration.alt: The friends celebrating as they finally see the real rainbow

metadata.illustrationStyle: Playful storybook illustration style. Rich saturated colors. Expressive characters with big eyes. Whimsical, like Pixar concept art.

questions.question: What three things do you need to see a rainbow?
choices: Sun, water, and the right spot
questions.explanation: Rainbows appear when sunlight shines through water droplets in the air, and you need to be in the right position to see them!

questions.question: Why couldn't Kai see the rainbow at first?
choices: A tree was blocking the sun
questions.explanation: The big tree's shadow was hiding the rainbow from Kai's view in his yard.

questions.question: How did the friends make their own rainbow?
choices: With a garden hose and water
questions.explanation: They used the garden hose to spray water in the sunlight, which created a rainbow in the mist!

questions.question: Who helped Kai solve the rainbow mystery?
questions.explanation: Kai's friends Zara and Marcus worked together with him to solve the mystery.

questions.question: What did Kai love most about rainbows?
questions.explanation: Kai knew all seven colors of the rainbow by heart: red, orange, yellow, green, blue, indigo, and violet!

pages.title: Fill in the Rainbow Colors
pages.instructions: Help Kai remember all the colors of the rainbow in order!
pages.text: The rainbow has seven colors: {0}, {1}, yellow, {2}, blue, {3}, and violet!

pages.title: Rainbow Recipe
pages.instructions: Complete the recipe for making a rainbow like Kai and his friends did!
pages.text: To make a rainbow, you need: bright {0} shining, {1} drops in the air, and the right {2} to see it from!
